methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Synhelion, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Gianluca Ambrosetti, is evaluated against a comparable set spanning 1 public company and 8 private peers in the renewable energy and climate technology sectors. The peer universe for direct stage-matched comparison comprises 4 Series C companies: KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Watershed ($1.8B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $80M+ total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yields $136M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $700M to $2.0B. The divergence between methods reflects uncertainty in whether Synhelion's capital efficiency and development trajectory align with higher-capitalized peers or represents an earlier position within the Series C band.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $116M to $1.6B, with a median implied value of $768M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around stage positioning within Series C and relative technological maturity relative to the peer set. Key sensitivities include proof-of-concept milestones, capital intensity of commercialization, and competitive positioning within concentrated climate-tech funding.
